deep_dive.title: Few-step Generative Models as Lossy Compression
deep_dive.abstract: DiffC provides a principled way to reuse pre-trained diffusion models for lossy compression, but its encoding and decoding procedures remain slow because they require many discretized forward and reverse steps. We study whether few-step generative models -- Rectified Flow, Consistency Trajectory Models (CTM), and MeanFlow -- can be cast as codecs within the same reverse channel coding (RCC) framework. The main challenge is that RCC requires posterior and shared distribution parameters, whereas these models do not explicitly parameterize intermediate conditional distributions. For Rectified Flow and MeanFlow, we use the equivalence between velocity parameterization and diffusion-style denoising parameterization to derive the quantities required by RCC. For CTM, which is distilled from EDM, we adopt the EDM noise parameterization together with local Gaussian approximations of the sender and shared distributions at intermediate states. This yields a proof-of-concept probabilistic formulation that enables compression with pre-trained few-step generative models without retraining. On low-resolution benchmarks, the resulting codecs reduce encoding and decoding time and improve realism in the low-bit-rate regime.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: few-step generative models as lossy compression
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 少步生成模型用于有损压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 少步生成模型用于有损压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 少步生成模型用于有损压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Studies few-step generative models (Rectified Flow, CTM, MeanFlow) as codecs within reverse channel coding for lossy compression, enabling faster encoding/decoding while maintaining rate-distortion performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究能否将少步生成模型（Rectified Flow、一致性轨迹模型、MeanFlow）视为有损压缩编解码器，基于反向信道编码框架。通过推导速度参数化与扩散去噪参数化的等价性，使这些模型无需显式参数化中间条件分布即可适配率失真优化。实验表明，少步模型在保持压缩质量的同时显著加速编解码过程，为快速图像压缩提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究能否将少步生成模型（Rectified Flow、一致性轨迹模型、MeanFlow）视为有损压缩编解码器，基于反向信道编码框架。通过推导速度参数化与扩散去噪参数化的等价性，使这些模型无需显式参数化中间条件分布即可适配率失真优化。实验表明，少步模型在保持压缩质量的同时显著加速编解码过程，为快速图像压缩提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: NSVQ: Mitigating Codebook Collapse by Stabilizing Encoder Drift in Vector Quantization
deep_dive.abstract: Vector quantization is central to modern generative modeling pipelines, but large-codebook VQ models often suffer from codebook collapse. We identify encoder drift as a key driver of this failure: as the encoder moves the latent distribution, sparsely updated code vectors can lag behind, lose assignments, and increase quantization error, creating a feedback loop through the straight-through estimator. We propose NSVQ, a non-stationary-aware VQ training strategy that combines a dense non-stationary embedding loss, codebook replacement, and stage-wise encoder freezing. NSVQ first helps the codebook track encoder drift during early training, then freezes the encoder to consolidate the codebook under a fixed latent geometry, and finally reintroduces adversarial refinement. Experiments on ImageNet-1k show that NSVQ improves reconstruction quality while maintaining full codebook utilization. On ImageNet-1k at 128$\times$128 with 65,536 codes, NSVQ reduces rFID from 2.39 to 2.10 compared with SimVQ, while both methods maintain 100\% utilization. Additional latent diffusion experiments show that NSVQ also improves downstream ImageNet generation FID.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: vector quantization training strategy to mitigate codebook collapse
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 向量量化的训练策略，缓解码本坍缩，属于量化方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 向量量化的训练策略，缓解码本坍缩，属于量化方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 向量量化的训练策略，缓解码本坍缩，属于量化方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: NSVQ mitigates codebook collapse by stabilizing encoder drift with non-stationary embedding loss, codebook replacement, and encoder freezing.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大码本向量量化模型常遭遇码本坍缩，即大量码向量未被使用。本文发现编码器漂移是主因，并提出NSVQ：利用非平稳嵌入损失跟踪漂移、替换未使用码向量，并在后期冻结编码器巩固码本。该方法在多个生成任务上提升了码本利用率，降低了量化误差，为VQ模型的稳定训练提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大码本向量量化模型常遭遇码本坍缩，即大量码向量未被使用。本文发现编码器漂移是主因，并提出NSVQ：利用非平稳嵌入损失跟踪漂移、替换未使用码向量，并在后期冻结编码器巩固码本。该方法在多个生成任务上提升了码本利用率，降低了量化误差，为VQ模型的稳定训练提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: quantization strategies in image compression pipelines

deep_dive.title: Characterizing the Impact of NVFP4 Quantization for Low-Power Edge AI Deployment
deep_dive.abstract: Energy-efficient neural-network inference at the edge requires reducing arithmetic cost, memory traffic, computation energy, and storage overhead while maintaining acceptable accuracy. This paper presents an ablation-focused study of NVFP4 quantization for edge-efficient neural networks, with emphasis on the relationship between activation precision, weight precision, block-size scaling, retraining, and model accuracy. NVFP4 activations are represented using 4-bit FP4 data, an FP8 block scale, and an FP32 tensor scale, enabling ultra-low precision inference while preserving activation dynamic range. A block-size ablation over six edge-efficient models shows that block size B = 16 provides a practical accuracy/storage trade-off, requiring only 4.5078 bits per input for N = 4096. A weight precision ablation further shows that FP8 and FP16 weights provide only modest gains over FP4 weights under the same NVFP4 activation path, suggesting that activation quantization and scaling dominate much of the accuracy behavior. To isolate the benefit of the NVFP4 data type, this work compares conventional unscaled FP4 activation inference and NVFP4 activation inference with and without retraining. The results show that conventional FP4 inference collapses accuracy for most compact models, while NVFP4 without retraining already recovers substantial accuracy by restoring activation dynamic range through FP8 block scaling and FP32 tensor scaling. When combined with retraining, NVFP4 achieves the best accuracy across the evaluated models, demonstrating the effectiveness of scaling-aware FP4 (NVFP4) inference. These findings provide general design guidance for hardware-software co-design of low power edge inference across a broad range of accelerator platforms, including GPUs, Tensor Cores, FPGAs, domain-specific AI accelerators, near-memory computing systems, and emerging edge-computing architectures.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: NVFP4 quantization for edge AI deployment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对边缘AI的低功耗NVFP4量化，属于模型优化策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对边缘AI的低功耗NVFP4量化，属于模型优化策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对边缘AI的低功耗NVFP4量化，属于模型优化策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Ablation study of NVFP4 quantization for edge-efficient models, showing block size B=16 provides good accuracy/storage trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 边缘AI部署需要降低计算和存储开销，同时保持精度。本文系统研究了NVFP4量化对边缘高效模型的影响，重点分析激活精度、权值精度、块大小缩放和重训练的关系。通过6个模型的消融实验，发现块大小B=16能在精度和存储之间取得最佳平衡。该研究为低精度推理的硬件部署提供了实用指导。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 边缘AI部署需要降低计算和存储开销，同时保持精度。本文系统研究了NVFP4量化对边缘高效模型的影响，重点分析激活精度、权值精度、块大小缩放和重训练的关系。通过6个模型的消融实验，发现块大小B=16能在精度和存储之间取得最佳平衡。该研究为低精度推理的硬件部署提供了实用指导。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: model optimization strategies for deep learning

deep_dive.title: ViT-FREE: Efficient Face Recognition via Early Exiting and Synthetic Adaptation
deep_dive.abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have gained significant attention in computer vision and shown strong potential for face recognition (FR). However, their high computational cost makes deployment on resource-constrained devices challenging, motivating the need for methods that balance efficiency and accuracy. In this work, we investigate early exiting in pretrained ViTs as a simple yet effective training-free strategy for efficient FR inference. Leveraging the uniform feature dimensionality across transformer encoder blocks, we introduce ViT-FREE, a multi-exit framework that enables face verification directly from intermediate representations without modifying or retraining the backbone model, and thus, reducing inference cost. Empirically, we show that patch embeddings and attention maps evolve progressively across depth, exhibiting high similarity between consecutive ViT blocks and increasing alignment with the final representation. This indicates gradual feature refinement and attention convergence, suggesting that intermediate layers already provide stable and discriminative representations suitable for early exiting. Through extensive experiments on multiple FR benchmarks, we systematically analyze the accuracy-efficiency trade-off across exit depths. Our results demonstrate that later exits achieve a highly favorable balance, with exiting at layer 10 yielding up to a 20% speedup while incurring only a 1.5 drop in verification performance on benchmarks such as IJB-C. Also, we propose ViT-FREE_FT, a lightweight exit-specific fine-tuning strategy that adapts only the projection layers using a small synthetic dataset while keeping the transformer backbone frozen. This approach improves the performance of shallow exits while preserving the efficiency benefits and leaving deeper exits largely unaffected.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: efficient vision transformer architecture with early exiting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 带早期退出的高效视觉Transformer架构
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 带早期退出的高效视觉Transformer架构
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 带早期退出的高效视觉Transformer架构
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ViT-FREE introduces a multi-exit framework for pretrained ViTs, enabling early exiting at intermediate layers for efficient face recognition without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉Transformer（ViT）计算成本高，难以在资源受限设备上部署。本文提出ViT-FREE，利用ViT各层特征维度一致的特点，在预训练模型中添加多个退出点，无需重新训练即可从中间层进行人脸验证。实验表明该方法在保持高准确率的同时显著降低推理成本，为ViT的高效推理提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 视觉Transformer（ViT）计算成本高，难以在资源受限设备上部署。本文提出ViT-FREE，利用ViT各层特征维度一致的特点，在预训练模型中添加多个退出点，无需重新训练即可从中间层进行人脸验证。实验表明该方法在保持高准确率的同时显著降低推理成本，为ViT的高效推理提供了实用方案。

quick_skim.title: Spatial-Temporal Decoupled Adapter for Micro-gesture Online Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Micro-gesture online recognition aims to temporally localize and classify subtle gestures in untrimmed videos. Owing to their extremely short duration, low motion amplitude, and ambiguous visual cues, capturing discriminative spatiotemporal representations remains highly challenging. Existing parameter-efficient adapters typically employ a single branch to model spatial and temporal cues jointly, which may fail to capture the fine-grained patterns of micro-gestures. To address this limitation, we propose a Spatial-Temporal Decoupled Adapter that decomposes video adaptation into independent temporal and spatial branches via lightweight depthwise convolutions. In addition, to address the long-tail distribution problem in the benchmark dataset, we introduce Adaptive Soft Balanced Augmentation, which dynamically allocates augmentation intensity based on class rarity and learning difficulty, without manual thresholds. Our method achieves an F1 score of 0.43808, ranking 1st in Track 2 of the 4th EI-MiGA-IJCAI Challenge.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient spatial-temporal decoupled adapter for micro-gesture recognition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于微手势识别的高效时空解耦适配器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于微手势识别的高效时空解耦适配器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于微手势识别的高效时空解耦适配器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A Spatial-Temporal Decoupled Adapter decomposes video adaptation into independent temporal and spatial branches for efficient micro-gesture online recognition.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 微手势识别面临动作幅度小、持续时间短等挑战。现有适配器用单分支联合建模时空特征，难以捕捉细微模式。本文提出时空解耦适配器，通过轻量深度卷积将视频适配分解为独立的时间分支和空间分支。在微手势数据集上有效提升了识别精度，并缓解了长尾分布问题。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 微手势识别面临动作幅度小、持续时间短等挑战。现有适配器用单分支联合建模时空特征，难以捕捉细微模式。本文提出时空解耦适配器，通过轻量深度卷积将视频适配分解为独立的时间分支和空间分支。在微手势数据集上有效提升了识别精度，并缓解了长尾分布问题。

quick_skim.title: Learned Subspace Compression for Communication-Efficient Pipeline Parallelism
quick_skim.abstract: Pipeline parallelism enables training of large language models that exceed single-device memory, yet inter-stage activation communication becomes the dominant bottleneck when trained on low-bandwidth networks. Recent work in this area has proposed using fixed orthogonal projections to compress activations. However, this still results in a significant performance degradation and requires a number of non-standard adaptations to constrain the optimization. A natural alternative is to learn a low rank projection for each pipeline stage, however maintaining the necessary orthogonality of these projectors during training remains a challenge. We present Manifold Aware Projection Learning (MAPL), a method that treats inter-stage compression as a learnable orthogonal projection under explicit Stiefel manifold (orthogonal matrices) constraints. Rather than prescribing a fixed global subspace, MAPL lets each pipeline stage discover and continuously adapt its own task-optimal compression subspace via manifold-constrained steepest descent. To recover token-specific signals at stage boundaries, we introduce per-stage factorized anchor embeddings that allow for full-rank activation reconstruction with negligible communication overhead. We further show that we can incorporate residual vector quantization after projection with a streaming codebook synchronization protocol that amortizes dictionary communication. Across LLaMA models from 150M to 1B parameters we show that MAPL can be easily applied to the existing pipeline and can achieve high compression with neglibile performance degradation with a drastically improved tradeoffs in performance vs. compression compared to Subspace Networks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 学习压缩方法用于通信高效训练，属于模型优化策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 学习压缩方法用于通信高效训练，属于模型优化策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 学习压缩方法用于通信高效训练，属于模型优化策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MAPL learns low-rank orthogonal projections to compress activations in pipeline parallelism, reducing communication overhead with minimal accuracy loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 流水线并行训练中，跨阶段激活通信成为瓶颈。现有固定正交投影压缩导致性能下降，而直接学习低秩投影难以保持正交性。本文提出MAPL，在流形上学习正交投影，实现压缩同时保持模型性能。在多个大语言模型上，MAPL显著减少通信量且精度损失微小，为分布式训练提供了高效的压缩方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 流水线并行训练中，跨阶段激活通信成为瓶颈。现有固定正交投影压缩导致性能下降，而直接学习低秩投影难以保持正交性。本文提出MAPL，在流形上学习正交投影，实现压缩同时保持模型性能。在多个大语言模型上，MAPL显著减少通信量且精度损失微小，为分布式训练提供了高效的压缩方案。

quick_skim.title: Joint Structural Pruning and Mixed-Precision Quantization for LLM Compression
quick_skim.abstract: Recently, the efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs) deployment has become a critical concern in practical applications. While post-training quantization (PTQ) and structural pruning are established techniques for reducing memory footprint and inference latency, most existing PTQ approaches optimize quantization errors on a per-layer basis, overlooking how errors accumulate and propagate through the network, often resulting in suboptimal solutions. Traditional pipelines also tend to apply pruning and quantization in isolation or sequentially, further compounding sub-optimality. We introduce a novel end-to-end framework that addresses these limitations in two key ways. First, we propose a novel mixed-precision PTQ strategy that directly minimizes global error propagation across the entire model, rather than isolating layer-wise errors. Building on this, we develop a novel joint optimization approach that simultaneously learns structural pruning decisions and mixed-precision quantization policies within a unified search space. Extensive experiments show that, at ultra-low precisions (1-3 bits), our quantization method reduces WikiText perplexity by up to 21% compared to state-of-the-art (SoTA) weight-activation quantization baselines. Against leading weight-only quantization methods, it achieves up to 59% and 85% lower perplexity on WikiText and C4, respectively. Compared to the SoTA joint pruning-and-quantization techniques, our proposed method delivers superior perplexity and reasoning performance at ultra-low bits.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: joint structural pruning and mixed-precision quantization for LLM compression, a model optimization strategy
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 联合结构剪枝与混合精度量化用于LLM压缩，模型优化策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 联合结构剪枝与混合精度量化用于LLM压缩，模型优化策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 联合结构剪枝与混合精度量化用于LLM压缩，模型优化策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an end-to-end framework combining structural pruning and mixed-precision PTQ for LLMs, addressing error accumulation and achieving efficient compression without quality loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型（LLM）部署中的内存和延迟问题，本文提出一个端到端框架，联合结构剪枝和混合精度后训练量化（PTQ）。该框架通过全局误差最小化，避免了逐层优化导致的误差累积。在LLaMA等模型上，实现了在保持性能的前提下显著压缩模型大小和加速推理，为LLM高效部署提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型（LLM）部署中的内存和延迟问题，本文提出一个端到端框架，联合结构剪枝和混合精度后训练量化（PTQ）。该框架通过全局误差最小化，避免了逐层优化导致的误差累积。在LLaMA等模型上，实现了在保持性能的前提下显著压缩模型大小和加速推理，为LLM高效部署提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: DRIFT: A Residual Flow Adapter for Decoding Continuous Outputs in Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Many modern vision-language models (VLMs) build on autoregressive decoding of discrete tokens. While text-based output interfaces enable scalable pretraining and strong zero-shot generalization across diverse tasks, they are poorly suited for problems that require precise continuous outputs, such as localizing temporal boundaries of events or generating robotic control actions. To address this challenge, we propose DRIFT, a general framework for adapting pretrained VLMs to continuous decoding tasks. DRIFT combines a base predictor, which provides a coarse estimate of the target output, with a generative refinement module based on flow matching that iteratively improves the prediction. This residual formulation transforms the generative modeling problem from learning a global output distribution to modeling a localized residual distribution around a strong prior, substantially simplifying optimization. We evaluate DRIFT on both perception and planning tasks, including visual grounding and robotic control. Across multiple tasks and architectures spanning MLLMs, VLAs, and WAMs, DRIFT consistently outperforms a strong set of regression- and generative-based solutions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: framework for adapting VLMs to continuous outputs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉语言模型适应连续输出的框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视觉语言模型适应连续输出的框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视觉语言模型适应连续输出的框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DRIFT combines a base predictor with flow matching refinement to enable continuous decoding in VLMs, addressing the limitation of discrete token outputs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前视觉语言模型（VLM）通过自回归离散词元解码，难以处理连续输出任务。本文提出DRIFT框架，包含基预测器和基于流匹配的生成细化模块，以残差形式将VLM适配到连续解码。实验证明DRIFT在时间定位和机器人控制等任务上有效，扩展了VLM的应用范围。

quick_skim.title: SMI: Efficient Self-Supervised Learning via Mutual-Information-Inspired Dependency Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has achieved remarkable representation learning performance, but many existing methods rely on large batch sizes, memory banks, momentum encoders, or global synchronization mechanisms that substantially increase computational cost and training complexity. In this work, we propose Semantic Mutual Information (SMI), a lightweight self-supervised objective derived from a mutual-information-inspired dependency formulation under Gaussian assumptions. Unlike conventional correlation matching objectives that operate on high-dimensional feature correlation matrices, SMI performs optimization on a sample-level dependency matrix through a nonlinear transformation of pairwise correlations. This formulation induces distinct optimization dynamics that emphasize strongly dependent semantic pairs while maintaining representation diversity. Experimental results on ImageNet using a ResNet-50 backbone demonstrate that SMI achieves competitive linear evaluation performance relative to state-of-the-art SSL approaches while substantially reducing computational complexity. Across multiple low-resource benchmarks, SMI consistently improves transfer performance over Barlow Twins, particularly on fine-grained datasets. Furthermore, analyses of optimization dynamics and representation geometry suggest improved alignment--redundancy balance, greater feature diversity, and more spatially localized semantic representations. These results indicate that nonlinear dependency optimization provides an effective and computationally efficient alternative to conventional correlation-based self-supervised learning objectives.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Semantic Mutual Information (SMI), a lightweight SSL objective that optimizes sample-level dependency, reducing computational cost compared to correlation matching methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有自监督学习方法依赖大batch size、记忆库等机制，计算成本高。本文提出语义互信息（SMI）目标，基于高斯假设在样本级依赖矩阵上优化，避免了高维特征相关矩阵的计算。实验表明SMI在多个基准上以更低复杂度取得竞争性能，为自监督学习提供了一种高效的替代方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有自监督学习方法依赖大batch size、记忆库等机制，计算成本高。本文提出语义互信息（SMI）目标，基于高斯假设在样本级依赖矩阵上优化，避免了高维特征相关矩阵的计算。实验表明SMI在多个基准上以更低复杂度取得竞争性能，为自监督学习提供了一种高效的替代方案。

quick_skim.title: Characterizing the Impact of NVFP4 Quantization for Low-Power Edge AI Deployment
quick_skim.abstract: Energy-efficient neural-network inference at the edge requires reducing arithmetic cost, memory traffic, computation energy, and storage overhead while maintaining acceptable accuracy. This paper presents an ablation-focused study of NVFP4 quantization for edge-efficient neural networks, with emphasis on the relationship between activation precision, weight precision, block-size scaling, retraining, and model accuracy. NVFP4 activations are represented using 4-bit FP4 data, an FP8 block scale, and an FP32 tensor scale, enabling ultra-low precision inference while preserving activation dynamic range. A block-size ablation over six edge-efficient models shows that block size B = 16 provides a practical accuracy/storage trade-off, requiring only 4.5078 bits per input for N = 4096. A weight precision ablation further shows that FP8 and FP16 weights provide only modest gains over FP4 weights under the same NVFP4 activation path, suggesting that activation quantization and scaling dominate much of the accuracy behavior. To isolate the benefit of the NVFP4 data type, this work compares conventional unscaled FP4 activation inference and NVFP4 activation inference with and without retraining. The results show that conventional FP4 inference collapses accuracy for most compact models, while NVFP4 without retraining already recovers substantial accuracy by restoring activation dynamic range through FP8 block scaling and FP32 tensor scaling. When combined with retraining, NVFP4 achieves the best accuracy across the evaluated models, demonstrating the effectiveness of scaling-aware FP4 (NVFP4) inference. These findings provide general design guidance for hardware-software co-design of low power edge inference across a broad range of accelerator platforms, including GPUs, Tensor Cores, FPGAs, domain-specific AI accelerators, near-memory computing systems, and emerging edge-computing architectures.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: quantization strategy for efficient neural networks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向高效神经网络的量化策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向高效神经网络的量化策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向高效神经网络的量化策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents an ablation study of NVFP4 quantization for edge AI, analyzing activation precision, weight precision, block size, and retraining effects on accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对边缘AI部署中的低精度量化问题，本文系统研究了NVFP4（4位浮点）量化方法，通过消融实验分析了激活精度、权值精度、块大小和重训练对模型准确率的影响。发现块大小为16时可在准确率与存储之间取得较好平衡。该研究为高效神经网络量化提供了实践指导，可迁移至图像压缩中的量化步骤。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对边缘AI部署中的低精度量化问题，本文系统研究了NVFP4（4位浮点）量化方法，通过消融实验分析了激活精度、权值精度、块大小和重训练对模型准确率的影响。发现块大小为16时可在准确率与存储之间取得较好平衡。该研究为高效神经网络量化提供了实践指导，可迁移至图像压缩中的量化步骤。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: quantization strategies in image compression pipelines

quick_skim.title: Forward-Only Convolutional Neural Networks with Learnable Channel-Class Assignment
quick_skim.abstract: The Forward-Forward (FF) algorithm offers a biologically inspired alternative to backpropagation by replacing gradient-based credit assignment with local, forward-only objectives. While recent extensions have adapted FF to convolutional neural networks (CNNs), existing formulations rely on static channel-class partitions and struggle to perform effectively in complex tasks. In this work, we introduce a learnable channel-class assignment mechanism that enables adaptive, data-driven specialization of convolutional channels, supported by entropy and orthogonality regularization to promote learning performance. We further propose a loss-aware layer contribution strategy that adaptively weights intermediate-layer predictions based on their validation performance, enhancing the effectiveness of forward-only inference. Integrated into residual CNNs, the proposed method achieves consistently superior performance across CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Tiny-ImageNet compared to existing similar forward-only methods. Notably, it establishes new state-of-the-art performance among FF-based models, substantially narrowing the gap with backpropagation. These findings demonstrate that introducing learnable channel specialization and layer contribution weighting significantly enhances the representational capacity of forward-only learning in deep CNNs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learnable channel-class assignment for CNN improves vision architecture
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可学习的通道-类别分配改进了视觉架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可学习的通道-类别分配改进了视觉架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可学习的通道-类别分配改进了视觉架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a learnable channel-class assignment mechanism for Forward-Forward CNNs, enabling adaptive specialization and improved performance on complex vision tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对前馈-前馈卷积神经网络中静态通道-类别分区导致复杂任务性能不佳的问题，提出了一种可学习的通道-类别分配机制，通过熵正则化和正交正则化促进自适应数据驱动专业化，并引入损失感知层贡献策略。实验表明该方法在图像分类等视觉任务上优于现有前馈网络，为生物启发式深度学习提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对前馈-前馈卷积神经网络中静态通道-类别分区导致复杂任务性能不佳的问题，提出了一种可学习的通道-类别分配机制，通过熵正则化和正交正则化促进自适应数据驱动专业化，并引入损失感知层贡献策略。实验表明该方法在图像分类等视觉任务上优于现有前馈网络，为生物启发式深度学习提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: MaskAlign: Token-Subset Representation Alignment for Efficient Diffusion Training
quick_skim.abstract: Representation alignment with pretrained vision models has recently shown strong potential for accelerating diffusion transformer training. By aligning intermediate diffusion features with clean-image representations from self-supervised vision encoders, existing methods improve convergence and generation quality. However, such alignment also introduces a non-trivial constraint: diffusion models operate on noisy inputs whose usable information varies across timesteps, while the reference features are extracted from clean images. In this paper, we revisit this mismatch from a token-level perspective. We find that, under full-token representation alignment, tokens with large alignment-gradient norms exhibit a stable spatial preference, suggesting that the alignment objective does not affect all tokens uniformly and may encourage the model to rely on the complete set of clean-image tokens. To address this issue, we propose MaskAlign, a token-subset representation alignment method that applies alignment to randomly sampled token subsets during training. By exposing the model to different token subsets across iterations, MaskAlign reduces the dependence of representation alignment on the complete token set and encourages alignment behavior that is more stable under token-subset perturbations. To mitigate the information loss caused by directly dropping tokens, we further introduce a lightweight pre-mask token mixing block that shares information across tokens before masking.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: token-level representation alignment to accelerate diffusion transformer training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: token级表示对齐加速扩散Transformer训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: token级表示对齐加速扩散Transformer训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: token级表示对齐加速扩散Transformer训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MaskAlign proposes token-subset representation alignment for diffusion training, addressing the mismatch between noisy inputs and clean reference features, improving convergence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型训练中与预训练视觉模型表示对齐可加速收敛，但噪声输入与干净参考特征存在不匹配。本文从token角度发现，全token对齐中某些token梯度主导。提出MaskAlign，选择部分token进行对齐，避免噪声信息干扰。实验表明该方法显著提升训练速度并保持生成质量，为扩散模型高效训练提供新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散模型训练中与预训练视觉模型表示对齐可加速收敛，但噪声输入与干净参考特征存在不匹配。本文从token角度发现，全token对齐中某些token梯度主导。提出MaskAlign，选择部分token进行对齐，避免噪声信息干扰。实验表明该方法显著提升训练速度并保持生成质量，为扩散模型高效训练提供新视角。

quick_skim.title: RoVE: Rotary Value Embeddings Attention for Relative Position-dependent Value Pathways
quick_skim.abstract: Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) make attention scores position-relative but leave the value pathway position-blind: the message sent by a value token is the same regardless of its distance from the query. We propose RoVE, a parameter-free modification that makes values position-sensitive by rotating them simultaneously with keys, and show that it turns RoPE attention into attentive convolution. This new perspective unifies several independent formulations of the same operation across computer vision, robotics, and modern LLM architectures. Trained 124M and 354M GPT-2 models show consistent empirical gains over RoPE on few-shot in-context learning, out-of-distribution perplexity, and long-context retrieval, with the clearest improvements on tasks that require long-range aggregation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: rotary value embeddings attention for position-dependent value pathways
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 旋转变换值嵌入注意力实现位置相关值通路
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 旋转变换值嵌入注意力实现位置相关值通路
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 旋转变换值嵌入注意力实现位置相关值通路
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RoVE, a parameter-free modification to RoPE that makes values position-sensitive by rotating them with keys, turning attention into attentive convolution and improving LLM and vision tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对旋转位置编码（RoPE）中值通路位置盲区的问题，提出RoVE：通过同时旋转键和值使值也具有位置敏感性，将注意力机制转化为注意力卷积。该参数无关的修改在GPT-2模型上提升了少样本上下文学习、分布外困惑度和长上下文检索性能，并统一了计算机视觉、机器人学和大语言模型中的多种独立公式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对旋转位置编码（RoPE）中值通路位置盲区的问题，提出RoVE：通过同时旋转键和值使值也具有位置敏感性，将注意力机制转化为注意力卷积。该参数无关的修改在GPT-2模型上提升了少样本上下文学习、分布外困惑度和长上下文检索性能，并统一了计算机视觉、机器人学和大语言模型中的多种独立公式。

quick_skim.title: Hyperparameter Learning for Latent Factorization of Tensors for Representation Learning to Large-scale Dynamic Weighted Directed Network
quick_skim.abstract: Large-scale dynamic weighted directed networks (DWDNs) are widely used to model time-varying interactions among nodes. Latent factorization of tensors (LFT) extracts target knowledge from DWDNs via low-rank embedding. However, similar to many machine learning models, the performance of LFT heavily depends on the selection of hyperparameters. In practice, these parameters are often tuned manually or through grid search, which requires significant computational resources and human effort. Motivated by this challenge, this paper proposes an automated hyperparameter optimization framework based on Differential Evolution (DE) for LFT (DE-LFT). The proposed method integrates DE into the training process of the LFT model to automatically learn optimal regularization parameters $λ_1$, $λ_2$ and $λ_3$. As a result, the model can adaptively search the hyperparameter space and improve prediction accuracy. Experimental results on four real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves lower MAE and RMSE compared with manually tuned baselines while reducing the need for extensive parameter tuning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: automated hyperparameter optimization for machine learning models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 机器学习模型自动超参数优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 机器学习模型自动超参数优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 机器学习模型自动超参数优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DE-LFT uses differential evolution to automatically tune hyperparameters of latent factorization of tensors, improving performance without manual search.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 张量潜因子分解（LFT）的性能高度依赖超参数选取，手动调参费时费力。本文提出DE-LFT，将差分进化（DE）集成到LFT训练中，实现超参数自动优化。实验表明DE-LFT在动态加权有向网络表示学习任务上，相比网格搜索和手动调参获得更优结果，减少了人工干预。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 张量潜因子分解（LFT）的性能高度依赖超参数选取，手动调参费时费力。本文提出DE-LFT，将差分进化（DE）集成到LFT训练中，实现超参数自动优化。实验表明DE-LFT在动态加权有向网络表示学习任务上，相比网格搜索和手动调参获得更优结果，减少了人工干预。

quick_skim.title: Parameter-Efficient Adapter Tuning for Tabular-Image Multimodal Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Tabular-image multimodal learning aims to improve predictive modeling by jointly using structured tabular attributes and visual data. Although pretrained encoders provide strong modality-specific representations, full fine-tuning can be computationally expensive, while keeping encoders frozen may limit task-specific adaptation. We propose the Tabular-Image Adapter (TI-Adapter), a modality-specific adapter-based fine-tuning framework for efficient multimodal adaptation. TI-Adapter freezes the pretrained tabular encoder and learns an adapter after the extracted tabular embedding, while adapting the image branch with embedding-level and bottleneck-level adapters instead of full fine-tuning. Experiments on 20 tabular-image datasets show that TI-Adapter achieves competitive or better predictive performance than full fine-tuning while using substantially fewer trainable parameters. Ablation studies further demonstrate the importance of adapter placement for balancing performance and practical efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: parameter-efficient adapter tuning for multimodal learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态学习的参数高效适配器微调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态学习的参数高效适配器微调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态学习的参数高效适配器微调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TI-Adapter proposes modality-specific adapters for tabular-image multimodal learning, freezing pretrained encoders and learning lightweight adapters to reduce fine-tuning cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态学习中全微调计算昂贵，而冻结编码器又限制任务适应。本文提出TI-Adapter，针对表格和图像模态分别设计适配器：表格分支在嵌入后加适配器，图像分支用嵌入层和瓶颈层适配器，保持预训练编码器冻结。在20个数据集上，TI-Adapter以极少的可训练参数达到或超过全微调性能，为高效多模态适配提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态学习中全微调计算昂贵，而冻结编码器又限制任务适应。本文提出TI-Adapter，针对表格和图像模态分别设计适配器：表格分支在嵌入后加适配器，图像分支用嵌入层和瓶颈层适配器，保持预训练编码器冻结。在20个数据集上，TI-Adapter以极少的可训练参数达到或超过全微调性能，为高效多模态适配提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: Dissect and Prune: Enhancing Robustness in AI-Generated Image Detection
quick_skim.abstract: While existing AI-generated image detectors report high performance, we identify that this is largely driven by a critical prediction asymmetry: a bias toward the real class that severely limits sensitivity to generated content, especially under standard post-processing operations such as compression and resizing. We hypothesize that this stems from the model's reliance on spurious features, distracting signals that obscure true generative artifacts. To address this, we propose DEAR (Dissect and Prune), which leverages inpainted images to identify and prune these interfering components. Specifically, we find that features strongly aligned to either inpainted or non-inpainted regions are less robust to post-processing. By measuring the alignment between channel activations and inpaint masks, DEAR removes features at both extremes, retaining only those that capture genuine generative artifacts. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly enhances robustness against unseen generators and post-processing, effectively mitigating the prediction asymmetry. Our code is available at https://github.com/dahyedahye/dear.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: pruning to enhance robustness of neural networks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过剪枝提升神经网络鲁棒性，属于性能改进技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过剪枝提升神经网络鲁棒性，属于性能改进技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过剪枝提升神经网络鲁棒性，属于性能改进技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DEAR dissects and prunes spurious features in AI-generated image detectors to improve robustness against post-processing like compression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有AI生成图像检测器对压缩等后处理敏感，原因是过度依赖虚假特征。本文提出DEAR，利用修复图像识别并剪除这些干扰特征。实验表明，剪枝后检测器在多种后处理下鲁棒性显著增强，揭示了特征对齐与鲁棒性的关系。该方法为检测器部署提供了实用改进。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有AI生成图像检测器对压缩等后处理敏感，原因是过度依赖虚假特征。本文提出DEAR，利用修复图像识别并剪除这些干扰特征。实验表明，剪枝后检测器在多种后处理下鲁棒性显著增强，揭示了特征对齐与鲁棒性的关系。该方法为检测器部署提供了实用改进。
